sections.paragraph:
πέτομαι (Hom.+; ins, pap, LXX; ParJer; ApcSed 8:3 p. 132, 28 Ja.; Philo, Gig. 6; Hecataeus: 264 Fgm. 21, 203 Jac. [in Jos., C. Ap. 1, 203]. S. πετάομαι.) w. the aor. ἐπετάσθην derived fr. πεταζω (cp. Aristot., HA 624b, 6 [εἰς πετάσθη]; also Is 11:14; JosAs 16 cod. A [p. 65, 11 Bat.]) fly Rv 4:7; 8:13; 12:14; 14:6; 19:17. Aor. 3 sg. GJs 9:1 v.l. (codd. Tdf. for ἐπεστάθη).—B. 682. DELG. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Hecataeus Hecataeus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. fr. = from
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

  1. sg. = singular

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 809
